what_it_does: Defines a browser-global ConceptV icon registry on window.CONCEPTV_ICONS, mapping icon names to metadata and SVG inner markup strings for rendering by a separate icon component.
observations: Each icon entry has a slug key plus set, cat, and body fields.
The file organizes icons into commented groups for core icons and extension sets such as status, UI, media, files, architecture, time, comms, and data.
SVG bodies are stored as template strings containing inline SVG child elements such as path, circle, rect, ellipse, and text.
The comments state shared rendering assumptions: 24x24 viewBox, currentColor stroke, default 1.5 stroke weight, rounded caps and joins, and fill none unless overridden.
Several icons intentionally use filled currentColor shapes or var(--cv-icon-bg, #fff) to create reversed foreground/background details.
Some icons include literal SVG text labels such as PDF, CSV, DOC, N, m, 2, x, y, and z.
standouts: The registry is data-driven rather than component-per-icon.
The icon vocabulary spans architecture, files, people, process, media, UI controls, status, data, and communication concepts.
The file carries both visual path data and semantic grouping metadata in the same object.
conventions: Entries use compact single-line object literals with aligned spacing in many sections.
The body field stores raw SVG inner content rather than parsed structures or functions.
Comment banners separate icon groups and document intended set values.
Color behavior relies on currentColor and the --cv-icon-bg CSS variable for contrast details.
concerns: The EXT-DATA section header does not correspond to a distinct ext-data set value; its chart icons use set "ext-arch" and related tag/link/pin icons use set "ext-ui".
The stated list of icon sets in the header does not mention an ext-data set despite a later EXT-DATA section heading.
Raw SVG markup strings are inserted as-is, so correctness and safety depend on controlled icon data and the renderer's insertion method.
notes: This file is the ConceptV icon path ledger: named icon metadata plus SVG body strings for a shared renderer.
questions: Is the EXT-DATA heading meant to introduce a separate set value or only a visual grouping inside existing sets?
Should text-based SVG labels be part of the icon style contract, or are they transitional path data?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file content: it acts as a source registry for SVG inner content and icon metadata, while rendering details are delegated to ConceptVIcon.
intention_signals: It appears intended to make a shared ConceptV icon language available through one browser-global data source while keeping rendering behavior centralized elsewhere.
novelty: A single plain JavaScript object combines categorized product-domain icons with generic UI and status controls, using raw SVG body strings as portable renderer input.
contribution: This file contributes a broad, named ConceptV icon vocabulary with set/category metadata and reusable SVG inner markup that a fused system would preserve as visual language data.
summary_for_embedding: Browser-global JavaScript icon registry defining ConceptV icon names, categories, set labels, and raw SVG body strings for a shared renderer.
intention_for_embedding: It was for centralizing ConceptV design-system icon path data so icons can be rendered consistently through a common component.
